title: agrostis polymorpha var. palustris
intro: Agrostis polymorpha var. palustris, also known as marsh bentgrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to North America. Traditionally, marsh bentgrass has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and soothing respiratory issues, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Marsh bentgrass can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
